relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul begins as servant, called and set apart for Divine good news.
The good news has roots in prophetic promise.
The Son enters David’s line according to flesh.
Resurrection marks him in power according to Spirit of holiness.
Grace and apostleship serve trust among all nations for the name.
Rome too is inside the call of Messiah.
Beloved holy ones receive grace and peace.
Their trust is announced through the world.
Paul’s spirit-service carries unceasing remembrance.
Prayer seeks a way opened by Divine will.
He longs to share strengthening spiritual gift.
Mutual trust becomes mutual encouragement.
He desires fruit among Rome as among the nations.
He is debtor to cultured and uncultured, wise and uncomprehending.
He is eager to announce good news in Rome.
The good news is Divine power for saving/healing to everyone trusting.
In the good news, Divine justice is unveiled from trust into trust.
Wrath is unveiled against truth held down by injustice.
What can be known of the Divine has been made manifest.
Creation bears witness to unseen power and divine nature.
Knowing without thanks darkens the heart.
Claimed wisdom can become foolishness.
Imperishable glory is exchanged for images of perishing life.
The handed-over heart dishonors the body.
Truth is exchanged for the lie when creature replaces Creator.
Distorted worship becomes distorted passion.
Paul names inflamed desire as part of the exchange-pattern he is indicting.
Refusing Divine knowledge unravels the mind.
Injustice fills the social field with envy, violence, deceit, and harm.
Speech and pride become engines of harm.
Understanding, covenant, affection, and mercy collapse.
The deepest distortion approves death-bearing patterns as normal.

contemplative_note: Romans 1 must be read inside Paul's larger trap-door argument: the indictment turns toward the judging reader in Romans 2, then toward universal accountability in Romans 3. Its mystical use is diagnosis of idolatrous exchange, not permission to target vulnerable groups.
